scope.summary: DI5 rejects an explicit timeout_seconds of zero at both buffered and streaming dispatch entry points before handler-specific parsing or Oracle I/O.
out_of_scope: Profile call_timeout_seconds=0 remains the separately documented no-timeout posture; this change only governs per-call arguments.
No registry schema change: timeout_seconds already advertised a minimum of 1.

entry_points.name: OracleDispatcher buffered dispatch
entry_points.trace: zero_timeout_is_rejected_before_normal_dispatch_reaches_oracle enumerates every registered tool whose public input schema advertises timeout_seconds; each receives InvalidArguments before the recording connection observes query or execute I/O.

entry_points.name: OracleDispatcher streaming query dispatch
entry_points.trace: zero_timeout_is_rejected_before_streaming_query_reaches_oracle invokes dispatch_stream for oracle_query and proves the same InvalidArguments envelope before recording-connection I/O.

limitations: Scoped verification only: clean-worktree oraclemcp unit tests for zero rejection and existing positive query/execute timeout override restoration; no live Oracle run.
A full workspace gate is not claimed: open G1 has an independently reproduced committed guard-test failure.

known_defects.description: G1's committed negative vector embedding batch test is red: a qualified app.VECTOR_EMBEDDING lookalike is Guarded instead of Forbidden because the parser-only normalizer matches after a dot.
